messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following private members club privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"The Club collects your name, address, date of birth, occupation, proposer details, and payment information as part of the membership application process. Successful applicants' details are circulated to the membership committee for approval. Member records are held for the duration of membership plus 5 years. We do not share your data with external parties. You may update your details by contacting the membership secretary."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) implied for membership management. Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) implied for circulation to the membership committee. Neither is explicitly stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Occupation and proposer details may reveal political, religious, or professional affiliations. No acknowledgement or safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Membership application, committee approval, and member record management are stated as purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Occupation and proposer details require justification as they go beyond standard membership administration data.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Membership duration plus 5 years is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only update of details (rectification) referenced. Other rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] "Do not share with external parties" is clearly stated. Internal committee circulation is disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Not applicable given no external sharing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (occupation/affiliation inference risk not addressed), Art. 5 (occupation and proposer data necessity not justified), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State explicit lawful basis for each processing activity
- Justify collection of occupation and proposer details and acknowledge potential affiliation inference
- Add a full data subject rights section
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
